name: Summit National Bank
description_short: Community bank serving Wyoming, Montana, and Idaho since 1984, offering checking, savings, mortgages, and agricultural lending with local branch locations.
description_long:
Summit National Bank was originally chartered in Wyoming as Hulett National Bank in 1984. The bank was founded on the principle that rural communities needed local financial infrastructure to support essential services like medical care, education, and retail commerce. The founders recognized that small towns across America required a dedicated community bank to serve as the backbone of local economic development. In 2004, Hulett National Bank unified its Wyoming branch names under the Summit National Bank brand, and has since expanded to include locations in Salmon, Idaho and Ekalaka, Montana.

Summit National Bank offers a comprehensive range of retail and business banking services. On the retail side, they provide checking accounts, savings accounts, money market accounts, and certificate of deposit (CD) products with competitive rates (6-month CDs at 3.64% APY and 9-month CDs at 3.55% APY as of the website snapshot). They offer Premier Money Market accounts with a 2.02% APY floor interest rate. Their lending products include mortgages, home equity lines of credit, personal lines of credit, auto loans, and business checking accounts. Digital banking conveniences include online banking, mobile banking apps, and digital payment options through Apple Pay, Samsung Pay, and Google Pay.

What distinguishes Summit National Bank is its explicit commitment to rural community development and local focus. The bank emphasizes being "Community Driven, Locally Focused" and positions itself as a "small town solution for big time ideas." Their primary lending areas—commercial, agriculture, real estate, mortgages, and construction—reflect their deep roots in agricultural and rural economies. The bank maintains physical branches in three states across the Northern Rocky Mountain region, suggesting stability and long-term commitment to these underserved markets rather than the consolidation typical of larger national banks.

Summit National Bank is best suited for individuals and businesses in Wyoming, Montana, and Idaho seeking local relationship banking with personalized service. However, potential customers should note that their geographic footprint is limited to three states and three physical locations, meaning they lack the branch network or digital-first convenience of larger national banks. While they offer modern banking conveniences, their website provides limited detail about fees, rate specifics, or minimum deposit requirements, which would require direct contact to evaluate competitiveness. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Money market accounts with 2.02% APY floor
pros: Competitive CD rates with 6-month APY at 3.64% and 9-month APY at 3.55%
Multiple digital banking options including mobile banking, Apple Pay, Samsung Pay, and Google Pay
Specialized agricultural lending reflecting deep rural community roots
Over 40 years of local banking history (since 1984) in the Northern Rocky Mountain region
Presence in three states (Wyoming, Montana, Idaho) with dedicated community focus
Comprehensive product suite including mortgages, home equity lines, business lending, and personal loans
Online banking and internet banking platforms for convenient account access
cons: Limited geographic footprint with only three physical branch locations across three states
Website lacks detailed fee schedules, minimum deposit requirements, or APR disclosures for loan products
No indication of 24/7 customer support or national availability like larger competitor banks
Limited transparency on rate competitiveness compared to national CD and money market offerings
Small bank status may mean fewer advanced financial products or investment services
best_for: Agricultural businesses and farmers in Wyoming, Montana, and Idaho seeking specialized lending
Rural residents seeking local relationship banking with community commitment
Small business owners in the Northern Rocky Mountain region needing commercial lending
CD and money market investors in their service areas looking for community bank rates
diagnosis: Summit National Bank is best for rural and agricultural customers in Wyoming, Montana, or Idaho who value local relationship banking and community focus over national branch networks. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—the bank only operates in three states with three total locations—making it unsuitable for customers requiring multi-state access or those outside their service areas.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
